hero.hero_overview: an angel with miraculous powers, a stark contrast to her fallen angel brother, Argus. Her role is to keep allies alive in team fights by healing them, providing buffs, and controlling enemies with slowing and damaging skills. While there are no specific "farming routes" for Support heroes like Rafaela, her gameplay focuses on roaming to support teammates, prioritizing healing, and using her Light of Retribution to poke enemies and secure assists
hero.hero_strength:
Rafaela can cast a special skill every 40s. After channeling for 2.5s, she instantly resurrects an ally and increases their Movement Speed by 30% for 5s (taking damage from heroes and Legend Creeps interrupts channeling and puts the skill on base cooldown).
This skill's cooldown is increased by 500% of the target's remaining respawn time, capped at 240s. This skill is not affected by Cooldown Reduction.
hero.hero_weakness: Rafaela's weaknesses include her extreme fragility and reliance on her teammates for protection, making her vulnerable to assassins and heavy damage in team fights. She also struggles in laning and early game due to limited damage and mobility, making her an easy target for aggressive opponents. Her kit's effectiveness is heavily tied to proper positioning, as misplacing herself can lead to her being instantly eliminated by enemy burst damage.

spell.spell_desc: Summon a Healing Spring that surrounds the hero and lasts 4s, restoring 2.5% Max HP every 0.4s to affected allied heroes (including self), and increasing Shield and HP Regen effects received by 25% (this effect cannot stack).

hero.hero_overview: Rafaela shines in the current patch as a reliable roamer and healer. Unlike other supports, she brings a constant speed boost that helps her team control the map early. The roaming passive also works in her favor, letting her move freely without falling behind in gold and experience. With her healing and map presence, she can keep her teammates safe while scouting dangerous areas.
hero.hero_strength: Her second skill is the core of her kit, giving both healing and speed boost that fit perfectly into the current meta. This makes rotations smoother and helps the team dodge crowd control or slows from the enemy. On maps like Broken Wall or when checking dangerous grass, her ability to reveal hidden threats is very valuable. Her ultimate provides an instant stun, which is a strong answer to aggressive roamers or junglers trying to initiate. In clutch moments, that stun can completely shut down an enemy dive and turn the fight around.
hero.hero_weakness: Rafaela becomes less effective if her team lacks a durable frontline, since she performs best when positioned behind a tanky EXP laner. Her presence in the early game is also limited, as she needs to reach level four quickly to unlock her ultimate before the first Turtle fight. Without it, her team may lose out in early skirmishes. She also struggles with high mana consumption in the first few minutes of the match. Building a Mana Necklace early helps her maintain consistent pressure and avoid running out of resources during crucial moments.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: She is great with tanky fighters who are not extremely fast-moving. And Lukas is an example. His hybrid defence bonus in his ultimate form can be greatly supported by Rafaela.
Rafaela is a great support for jungler Roger. They can be together a duble trouble in late game.
Rafaela can take a safe position behind him while also healing him and other teammates.
I believe they can be a great duo too.
Do you know they are twins in the lore? They can together work too.
Hanabi is a marksman that can protect herself from CC effects and this can be greatly supported by Rafaela. Their ultimate skills are compatible together.
Miya can safely attack while Rafaela and the fighter is protecting her from danger.
An overfed Uranus with the boosts of Rafaela May learn how to fly!

Fanny is an extremely mobile hero which uses hit and run strategy while Rafaela excels at prolonged teamfights. Since it is impossible for Rafaela to keep up with the speed of Fanny, Rafaela's boosts will be completely useless for Fanny.
Just similar to Fanny, combining this two is a bad idea too.
Hayabusa is also extremely fast for Rafaela.
Joy is also fast for Rafaela.
Guinevere synergizes better with a settler tank rather than a healing support.

If Hylos is in the exp lane or in the Jungle, these two will flawlessly protect the damage carry heroes in the team , making them almost immortal. 

Additioannly, their skillsets are greatly compatible.
Similar to Hylos. But as some assassins were roo fast for her, Belerick May be in some situations too slow for her.
Cici is fast for Rafaela, also Cici's CC is not enough for Rafaela to even support it more with her ultimate and slow.

Chou is fast and he does not need healing support. His aim is to utilize hit and run  strategy.

Also, if you pick Chou in exp lane and Rafaela in roam at the same time, there will be no proper tanks in the game.
They can set up an aggressive gameplay strategy.
Kadita can safely land her combo after Rafaela's ultimate.
Pharsa and other long range mages similar to her needs settler tanks rather than healing supports.
She can not always ensure Saber's safety.
Similar to Fanny , Ling and Hayabusa.
They can dominate the game with a strong mage.
Irıthel is way too fast for her .
They May habe a good potential.
This could make a great duo.
It is almost impossible for them to land a proper combo together.
Just no.
This combo deserves a try. Rafaela May greatly boost his chasing abilities and also protect him from burst damage.

Probably not gonna work.

I have never seen this combo before but I think it can work!

Selena can secure low HP kills after Rafaela and the ally exp laner engages in a fight. But this combo will probably depend on the skills and experience of the Selena player.

Natalia is one of the best heroes to hunt down marksmans , you can save your marksman from this deadly hunter with Rafaela.
Rafaela can disrupt his combos from a safe distance. However, Khufra can serve his teammates better positions than Rafaela does.
Atlas will easily outshine her due to the fact that his skillset is a lot way stronger than hers in late game.

You can help your allies to catch Valentina fast.

It is hard for Rafaela to keep up with Cecillion due ro his ultimate and second skill.
Rafaela's allies need to stay close to Rafaela to inherit her boosts. However, this may be a great chance for Nana to outplay everyone.
Kadita can dive in to catch Rafaela and burst her down.
Roamer Badang can outshine her.
Rafaela can lock Karına and save her allies from Karına.
Rafaela can disrupt his ultimate .
Freya needs to be locked down with high CC Rather than being slowed down.
Layla's extreme late game range can challenge Rafaela.
Rafaela can be a good choice against Bane.
Minsittihar can surprise Rafaela with his hooking skill.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Always start with helping your jungler. Start with skill 1 and hit the buffs with your jungler. Be careful not to steal it. 

You can also zone out opponent tanks such as Franco and Tigeral to prevent them from invading your jungle and bothering your jungler.

Follow your jungler and help him/her during his/her first gank. Always do this. Also, before that gank, specifically, after your jungler takes the first buff and heads off to the second, you can help your mage to push the midlane. Meanwhile, your jungler will take the second buff safely. Then as a Trio, you can try your first gank to one of the solo lanes.

Help your team in the first turtle fight.

Until Mid game, if everything is OK, you can cling to your marksman. You two can dominate the Gold lane in mid game together. 

You can also wander around the map with your mage to surprise the opponents with sudden gangs.

Don't forget to check bushes with your first skill against possible ambushes. Rafaela's first skill can reveal hidden enemies inside the bushes.

Focus on supporting your fighter and protecting your marksman.

Be sure that you are behind your fighter but in front of your marksman and mage. 

Try to hit multiple enemies with your first skill, it could result good . 

Use revitalize spell wisely at the rightest moment. 

You can use it when:

Your fighter gets focused. You can use revitalize+your 2nd skill to give him/her a survival and outplay chance.

Your marksman is targetted by one-shot heroes. Again combine it with your second skill. 

In addition , you can aggressively use your ultimate in late game on enemy assassins to protect your allies or lock them since it will have a lower cooldown compared to the early stages of the game.

While playing Rafaela, you always need to know how to safely poke your enemies and give them the highest damage possible. Even if she is a support, her damage is still better than tanks. 

Maximizing her poking abilities can help greatly in the early stages of the game.

There is an important note that you definitely need to consider:

Rafaela is not a tank. Giving her all the responsibility to take damage of the opponent team is not a healthy idea.

She definitely needs a fighter/tank hero which she can depend on. This is the way that she can reveal her maximum potential.

Why we don't combine her with agressive early game heroes and invade enemy jungle.

Imagine Hylos Jungle, Rafaela roam and Luo-Yi mid lane are invading your jungle in the beginning of the game. 

Isn't this scary?

I believe she may be played as a midlaner with a tough tank.

But being loyal to her original build would be important despite not being a roamer.

At least, she would still need Flask of the Oasis.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Understandable from the name, "Support Emblem" is the best choice for a support hero, and so for Rafaela. It increases her healing, movement speed and cooldown reduction.

Inspire is chosen to support her cooldown reduction coming from the emblem itself.(10+5=15)

Tenacity is preferred to ensure her safety . Because most of the time she needs to get closer to her opponents to poke and stun them.

Focusing Mark is great for her since she is a support and it is so easy for her to activate it. Granting your allies +6% damage for 3 seconds is a crazy contribution.
emblems.emblem_title: The emblem set a support needs

equips.equip_desc:
Demon Boots is used to give her mana regen. So she won't have to worry about her mana during teamfights.

Use the healing option of roam boots. 

Flask of the Oasis is obligatory item in current meta for every healing support.

Buy Oracle to counter anti-heal items and to gain hybrid defence.

Buy Dominance Ice to implement anti-heal and attack speed reduction on your enemies.

Buy Athena's Shield to avoid getting one-shotted by enemy mid laners or magic assassins.


Use Ice Queen Wand to slow down enemies during teamfights, so landing your ultimate will be easier.


When you combine:

Healing roam boots+ Flask of Oasis + Oracle + Revitalize + 2nd skill , you can make your allies close to immortal.


Flask of the Oasis, Oracle and Dominance Ice are her core items. Always and definitely buy Flask of the Oasis first, but you can swipe the places of Oracle and Dominance Ice depending on the game, (usually buying Oracle first is better).


Last two items can change according to the enemies.

If the opponent's have continuous magic damage heroes you can buy Radiant Armor instead of Athena's Shield.

If they have physical burst, you can buy Antique Cuirass.

You can also prefer Immortality or Winter Throne in the late game.

By the way, your cooldown reaches up to +35% with this build, which is enough for her. Rafaela does not need to reach maximum cooldown of 45% like Faramis or Angela.

Still,you can complete it to 40% with any defensive item that gives you cooldown reduction.

You can do it to 45% with Enchanted Talisman. However, this item would be a slot waste for her for most of the time.

hero.hero_overview: Rafaela is one of the oldest supports in the game. She is often ignored, but she can shine with a right selection of ally heroes.
hero.hero_strength: She can slow down the opponent team while buffing allies movement speed. Her ultimate has a low cooldown and it can counter several heroes decisively.
hero.hero_weakness: She is weak against burst damage. Especially, in the early game, since she does not have any defensive items properly, it is quite easy to one shot her with assassins.

spell.spell_desc:
Revitalize spell will often help you and your teammates in critic situations. Sometimes her heal from 2nd skill won't be enough. At that time , revitalize can be a Real clutch to depend on.

In addition, she can also cast it to tank more damage or rescue herself from danger.


Depending on the games, vengeance and flicker may be other possible options.

hero.hero_overview: Rafeela She is considered one of the old heroes in the game, but she is almost marginalized because most people do not realize the benefit of her strength, although she is considered one of the best supporters.
hero.hero_strength:
Raffaella has four  characteristics: 
1-The first skill has the benefit of slowing down fast enemies and also reveals heroes who rely on invisibility such as Amon, Natalia and Leslie, which seems very special. 

The second skill has two benefits. 
2-Speed up allies and smuggle them from enemies 
3-Increase your maximum health points
4-The last skill gives temporary freeze to the enemy. 

All these benefits and some still consider them weak 
The right ally for Rafaela in the game is Frederin and Bartis because their life points are high. 
It is preferable to use armored equipment so that you do not die quickly.
hero.hero_weakness: Rafaella's weakness is that she cannot stand up to heroes that deal constant damage, especially in the late game. She becomes somewhat weak and easy to kill. Among the heroes that Rafaella should not be used against are (Benedetta Lylia Louyi Youzong)

spell.spell_desc:
- Its a good pair with Rafaela being squishy to avoid Aoe/CC skills (Ex. Gatotkaca Ult, Yve Ult, Or Odette Ult.) pairing it with 2nd skill will be a chef's kiss in various Crowd Control Scenarios
- Honorable Mention for: Revitalize (As being used for sustain Rafaela, good for guarding /supporting your allies)

dominants.dominant_desc:
At the start of the game you should follow the jungler spot and help him to take down the buff atleast 30 second after that go to the mid lane and help your midland to clear and hit the enemy.
(It depends:After clearing the lane follow the midland if where she go rather gold or midland) (if midlander not rotate you must follow the jungler and help him to farm after that 1:30 you must go to the gold lane and help him to take down the enemy an dgo again to the midland and help the midlane.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Support emblem is for cd reduction and high healing effect.
Agility extra movement (Easy rotate)
Pull yourself together extra CD reduction (2cd reduction)
Impure rage (Extra damage this is advantage for you teammates to trigger the magical defense to enemy this is combine for your 1st skill)

equips.equip_desc: This equipment use for high healing effect and slow effect.

hero.hero_overview:
Rafaela is one of the unpopular hero but she has a great skill's for team fight. To use rafaela always remember that you position is always on back of the other hero's and keep zoning that make sure that your second skill is still reach them.
Rafaela first skill is advantage for checking bush and slow the enemy if you chase the enemy. Rafaela second skill have healing effects and speed up best for clash, last one is rafaela ultimate, rafaela ultimate have stun and Burt's damage. Her passive is B
hero.hero_strength: Rafaela strength will base on your teammates if your jungler have a best skill and good gameplay 90% you will win.
hero.hero_weakness: Hero weakness is fighter, assasin, long range mm. For example alucard, ling, Hayabusa and burst damage hero.
